title: centaurea minima
intro: Centaurea minima, also known as the lesser knapweed or dwarf knapweed, is a low-growing perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, yellow or pink flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The leaves and flowers of Centaurea minima have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to their antibacterial, antifungal, and antioxidant properties. Centaurea minima can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
